svg.title: samaydlette.com authorization boundary with data flows and FIPS modules
svg.desc: A FedRAMP authorization-boundary diagram for samaydlette.com. Shows the cloud service offering's internal components (the static site and continuous-compliance plane, plus the Cognito-gated Silk Reeling application sub-system), services leveraged from AWS under FedRAMP Certified Class C package AGENCYAMAZONEW, external services without a separate FedRAMP authorization that are inside the boundary per Rule of Thumb #2, out-of-boundary items per Rules of Thumb #3 and #4, the eight major data flows lettered A through H with ingress and egress points, FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptographic modules in scope, and a legend.
svg.text: Per FedRAMP “A FedRAMP Authorization Boundary” guidance · CSO targeting Rev 5 Moderate / 20x Class C
Per Key Concept #6: external services with a pre-existing FedRAMP authorization, inherited under the customer responsibility matrix.
VPC networking / KMS HSM / IAM control plane / S3 substrate / CloudFront edge / Lambda host / API Gateway / Cognito / Secrets Manager / EventBridge / ACM PKI / CloudTrail
Reached from CI via the AWS service APIs (HTTPS 443, TLS 1.2+, AWS SigV4). All deploys traverse this band.
FedRAMP certification package: AGENCYAMAZONEW (AWS East/West, FedRAMP Certified Class C)
Per Key Concept #1 + Rule of Thumb #1: components the CSP controls, plus the “federal information” surface (here scoped to the published artifacts at /.well-known/).
Public CDN; TLS 1.2+; OAC to S3
cdn_distribution · egress edge to public Internet
aws_route53_zone · DS record published at registrar
SSE-S3 (AES-256); public access blocked; read via CloudFront OAC only
signs runtime signal (KMS ECDSA); writes ksi-signal-runtime.json
rate(1 day) trigger for the emitter Lambda
Deployer: GitHub OIDC → STS, no static keys (POAM-001 closed)
Emitter role (non-priv): read S3/CF config, DescribeSecret, KMS Sign; write 1 S3 key
identity_provider · SRP auth, hosted UI, short-lived JWTs
Cognito JWT required on /api/*; throttled 20 rps / 10 burst
api_gateway · rejects unauthenticated calls at the edge
Serves /api; calls Anthropic for feedback text
function · Node.js · no VPC · pose extraction runs client-side
Anthropic API key for the app Lambda
secrets_manager · customer-CMK encrypted · rotation checked daily
kms_key · on the inherited AWS KMS FIPS 140-3 HSM
/.well-known/ OSCAL SSP, POA&M, KSI signal, VDR
the “federal information” surface in scope; Sigstore-signed
External services without FedRAMP ATO — in boundary per Rule of Thumb #2
Each affects the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of system artifacts. Per Key Concept #5 + ROT #2, these are inside the boundary even with no separate FedRAMP authorization.
Issues short-lived X.509 cert per CI run
Second factor on AWS root + operator IAM user
CIA impact: privileged-account auth · hardware token planned (POAM-025)
GitHub Advisory DB · CISA KEV catalog
App Lambda's outbound AI call for feedback
CIA impact: app egress · interconnection tracked as POAM-020 (Bedrock migration planned)
No federal data in this PoC
The Anthropic interconnection carries only app feedback prompts today; no federal information is in scope.
If federal data ever enters the system, the Anthropic egress is reassessed before any such data could flow.
FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptographic modules in scope
Required by the FedRAMP Authorization Boundary Diagram convention. Lists the cryptographic modules that protect system data and metadata in transit and at rest.
• AWS KMS — FIPS 140-3 (HSM-backed); the validated module behind all four customer-managed CMKs below
• ACM TLS termination on CloudFront — FIPS-validated cipher suites; TLS 1.2+ enforced
• S3 SSE-S3 — AES-256, AWS-managed keys, on the public website + access-log buckets (appropriate for public data)

text.tspan: Customer-managed CMKs (keys on the inherited KMS HSM):

svg.text: • at-rest — AES-256-GCM; encrypts CloudWatch Logs + Lambda env
• app-secret — encrypts the Anthropic API key in Secrets Manager
• runtime-signing — ECDSA P-256; signs the daily runtime signal (pubkey published)
• DNSSEC KSK — ECDSA P-256 (us-east-1); signs the Route 53 zone
External, no FedRAMP ATO: Sigstore Fulcio + Rekor use ECDSA P-256 (FIPS-approved algorithm); the implementations are not FIPS-validated modules. Trust is anchored in the public Rekor log.
Algorithms: AES-256-GCM (at rest) · ECDSA P-256 (Sigstore, runtime + DNSSEC signing) · SHA-256 (artifact hashing)
TLS: 1.2+ on all public endpoints (CloudFront, API Gateway, AWS APIs, GitHub, Sigstore, Duo, Anthropic)
Data at rest: CloudWatch Logs + Secrets Manager via customer CMKs; S3 buckets via SSE-S3 (AES-256)
Local laptop where code is authored.
Out per Rule of Thumb #4 (dev environment without federal info).
Out per Rule of Thumb #3 (corporate services not affecting CIA).
Anyone fetching the site or verifying a
bundle; app users authenticate via Cognito.
Consumers of public artifacts + authenticated app end-users.
Conceptually in scope at any future point a customer is onboarded.

g.text: Authorization boundary (red dashed border per FedRAMP convention)
Leveraged services with FedRAMP certification (sp-inherited; AGENCYAMAZONEW, Class C)
External services without FedRAMP ATO — in boundary per Rule of Thumb #2
Out of boundary (Rule of Thumb #3 corporate services and #4 dev environments)
GitHub OIDC → STS role; no static keys (priv.)
In transit TLS · at rest SSE-S3
CSR, ECDSA P-256 signature, log entry
App user → API Gateway → app Lambda → Anthropic
Cognito JWT (end-user); Lambda holds API key
